items.action: Create: Build a Motion variable collection in Figma with duration and easing tokens matching the code definitions (fast01: 70ms, fast02: 110ms, moderate01: 150ms, moderate02: 240ms, slow01: 400ms, slow02: 700ms). Alternatively, create a documentation page in the Figma file declaring the code-side motion token values and linking to packages/motion.
items.value_framing: Motion specifications exist in code but are invisible from Figma. An agent building from Figma alone will produce static components with no motion guidance, requiring post-generation correction on every animated interaction.

items.action: Move: Port functional intent descriptions from code-side MDX overview paragraphs to Figma COMPONENT_SET root entries for all 45 primary components. Start with the 10 most-used components (Button, Data Table, Dropdown, Text Input, Accordion, Modal, Notification, Tabs, Checkbox, Toggle).
items.value_framing: Without component descriptions, an AI agent cannot determine component purpose from the Figma API. Every agent interaction requires manual lookup, adding correction cycles to every design-to-code handoff.

items.action: Create: Write descriptions for Figma variable definitions, prioritising Theme semantics (548 vars) and Colors primitives (135 vars). At minimum, add collection-level descriptions and descriptions for the 20 most-consumed semantic tokens (text-primary, background, layer-01, support-error, interactive, border-strong, etc.).
items.value_framing: Without token descriptions, an AI agent cannot distinguish tokens by purpose. Token selection errors propagate to every component generated, requiring theme rework on each affected component.

items.action: Create: Build a parity gap register as a structured JSON file documenting: (1) known architectural differences between Figma mode-based variables and code flat JS exports, (2) intentional omissions (motion in code only, grid styles in Figma only), (3) naming convention mapping (Figma slash-paths to code camelCase/kebab-case).
items.value_framing: Without a gap register, automated drift detection generates false positives on 1682 tokens. Every parity check requires manual triage, blocking CI/CD integration of token sync tooling.

items.action: Rework: Add YAML frontmatter to MDX documentation files with structured fields: component_name, purpose, category, status, related_components. Apply to all 126 component MDX files. Auto-fixable with a script.
items.value_framing: Without structured metadata, an agent must parse prose to extract component purpose and status. Frontmatter enables instant structured lookup, reducing per-component context processing.

items.action: Create: Build a naming convention mapping as a structured JSON file documenting Figma slash-separated paths and their code camelCase/kebab-case equivalents. This enables automated parity checking without value-based fallback.
items.value_framing: Without a naming map, every token parity check must fall back to value matching. Name-based matching is faster, more reliable, and catches value drift that value-only matching misses.

items.action: Rework: Add Error handling and Edge cases sections to the 10 most-used component MDX files (Button, Data Table, Dropdown, Text Input, Accordion, Modal, Notification, Form, Select, Tabs). This completes levels 5 and 6 of the documentation hierarchy.
items.value_framing: Missing error handling and edge case documentation means agents produce components that work for happy paths but fail on boundary conditions. Each edge case failure requires a correction cycle.

items.action: Create: Publish tokens in DTCG (Design Token Community Group) format alongside the existing JS exports. This enables standard tooling interop (Style Dictionary, token transformers) without replacing the JS-primary workflow.
items.value_framing: Custom JS token format requires Carbon-specific extraction logic. DTCG format would enable any standard token tool to consume Carbon tokens without custom parsing, reducing integration effort for downstream teams.

items.action: Rework: Update code-side component tokens (packages/themes/src/component-tokens/) to alias semantic theme tokens instead of hardcoding hex values. This completes the three-layer alias chain in code, matching the Figma architecture.
items.value_framing: Broken alias chain at the component level means an agent following token references from component to primitive hits a dead end in code. Theme changes require manual hex updates in component-tokens/ instead of cascading automatically.

items.action: Rework: Audit all components for token binding completeness. Use Text Input/Accordion binding depth as the target standard (colour + typography + spacing + contextual layer). Prioritise the 10 most-used interactive components.
items.value_framing: Inconsistent token binding means an agent generates theme-aware code for some components but hardcoded values for others. This creates per-component rework when switching themes.

items.action: Create: Build empty state variants for data-dependent components (Data Table, Dropdown, List, Contained List) or create a reusable EmptyState component pattern with documentation on when and how to use it.
items.value_framing: Without empty state patterns, every data-dependent view requires custom empty state design. This is a design quality gap that affects end-user experience and adds design debt to every consuming team.

items.action: Create: Add skeleton/loading state variants to async-capable components (Data Table, Dropdown, Select) or document that the Loading component should be used as a wrapper pattern for these cases.
items.value_framing: Inconsistent loading patterns mean agents must decide per-component whether to use a dedicated skeleton or a wrapper Loading component. Documented patterns eliminate this ambiguity.

items.action: Rework: Standardise typography binding in Figma -- choose either individual variable bindings or composite text styles as the canonical approach, and apply consistently across all components. Document the chosen approach.
items.value_framing: Inconsistent typography binding means an agent extracting type styles gets different data shapes from different components. Standardisation enables reliable automated typography extraction.
